topic: Part 6.13 The MOND Grouping — A Category-Error Import
our_argument_summary: p6-613-mond-grouping argues that five WINs (WIN-047/048/052/054/055) added in the March 23, 2026 commit burst trace item-for-item to the MOND community canonical anomaly list and were imported without dome-internal derivation. The kernel-of-truth h3 (introduced by EXP-427, tightened by EXP-428) concedes that the dome author had a coherent sub-category available — specifically, that WIN-048 is explicitly labeled "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" — and that filing the MOND-grouping items as confirmed WINs rather than under this sub-category is the structural self-own. The closed-world h3 (h3#3) argues that even attempting derivation from disc-cosmology principles is dimensionally impossible.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — section-level review, no per-WIN code pipeline at this level.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: CRITICAL: Our wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence opens with "The dome categorizes this as a 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction'" — this attribution is not supported by live dome content. Verified by direct fetch of wins.html (2026-05-19): WIN-048 is titled "CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction" with badge CONSISTENT; the dome categorizes it as a positive ECM prediction (ΔT/T ∝ E_solar_circuit × cos(θ_ecliptic), aligned to ecliptic plane). The phrase "Counter-Prediction" does not appear anywhere on the page. Audit.html (the dome's canonical claim taxonomy) lists five claim classes — Prospective confirmed / Retrospective structural / Supportive-nonunique / Pending-contested / Open refinement — no "Counter-Prediction" sub-category exists in dome taxonomy.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review, not per-WIN. The MOND-grouping WINs themselves: WIN-048 has no monitor.py entry (per existing globe-fingerprints/WIN-048.json code_search.monitoring_present: false). Live-power monitor includes "CMB Axis of Evil" with predicted >2σ / observed 2.5σ — that's an aggregate threshold check, not a derivation pipeline.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The MOND grouping items are imports from the MOND research program, not relabels of standard physics. Standard ΛCDM cosmology does not predict the alignment, RAR scatter pattern, El Gordo mass, etc. — the items are genuine ΛCDM anomalies. The dome imports them with positive ECM framing (not as relabels).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: All five MOND-grouping WIN observations (RAR, El Gordo mass, CMB axis, Hubble tension, Cepheid tension) were published by mainstream researchers years before the dome adopted them as WINs in the 2026-03-23 commit burst. Tegmark/de Oliveira-Costa Axis of Evil 2003, McGaugh RAR 2016, Asencio El Gordo 2023, etc.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome-internal derivation exists for any of the five MOND-grouping items. Section h3#2 paragraph 3 documents this directly: "There is no derivation chain from the disc radius to RAR's a₀ threshold. There is no dome-mechanical argument for why the El Gordo cluster's mass anomaly should be 6.2σ." The dome supplies positive ECM-style framing (e.g., for WIN-048: "ΔT/T ∝ E_solar_circuit × cos(θ_ecliptic)") without dimensional derivation.

holes_found.description: Kernel's load-bearing "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-label citation is FACTUALLY WRONG. The dome does not use this phrase anywhere on its published wins.html (verified by direct fetch 2026-05-19 of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html). The dome's WIN-048 is titled "CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction" with status "CONSISTENT" and frames the alignment as a positive ECM prediction (formula ΔT/T ∝ E_solar_circuit × cos(θ_ecliptic)) — not as a counter-prediction. The dome's audit.html publishes the canonical claim taxonomy as five classes (Prospective confirmed / Retrospective structural / Supportive-nonunique / Pending-contested / Open refinement) — no "Counter-Prediction" sub-category exists in the dome's taxonomy. Our wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence opens with "The dome categorizes this as a 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction'" — this attribution is incorrect and is the upstream source of the false claim. The kernel-of-truth concession ("the author had the right concept and used it once") rests on this false premise; without the sub-label, there is no "right concept the author had-and-only-used-once" — the entire kernel-of-truth move collapses. Affected text: (a) data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence opening sentence; (b) sections.json p6-613-mond-grouping kernel h3 paragraph 1 (the "explicitly labeled 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction'" clause); (c) sections.json p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3 paragraph 2 (the "explicitly labeled by the dome as a 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction'" clause); (d) sections.json p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3 paragraph 3 (the "dome's own 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-framing — at least internally consistent..." clause). A dome defender pointing out the sub-label doesn't exist could discredit the kernel-of-truth concession AND the self-own argument that builds on it.
holes_found.recommendation: Two-part fix. (1) Correct data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence — the dome does NOT use "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" as a sub-label. Reword to reflect the dome's actual framing: WIN-048 is titled "CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction" with badge CONSISTENT; the dome claims a positive ECM prediction (alignment with ecliptic) AND a ΛCDM-failure framing in the same WIN ("no standard-model explanation"; the "Dome vs Globe" panel: "Standard ΛCDM predicts statistical isotropy — no mechanism for ecliptic alignment"). The argument we want is: the dome's prose pairs positive ECM claims with ΛCDM-trouble claims; the load-bearing weight is on the ΛCDM-trouble half because the dome supplies no dimensional derivation for the ECM half. (2) Restructure p6-613-mond-grouping kernel h3 to drop the false sub-label claim. Two options: (Option A) restructure the kernel-of-truth to credit the dome's mixed framing pattern — the dome does sometimes signal that an item is more anti-ΛCDM than pro-ECM (WIN-054's actual title is "El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility" — that IS a counter-prediction-style framing, in the dome's words, just using "Impossibility" instead of "Counter-Prediction"). The kernel could be rebuilt around WIN-054's title as the demonstration that the dome has the rhetorical category available. (Option B) drop the kernel-of-truth h3 entirely and lead with the list-match argument (h3#2). The kernel's function is rhetorical (acknowledge what the dome got right before attacking); if no defensible kernel exists, the section can open with the borrowing argument. Recommend Option A — preserves the kernel function, uses a verifiable dome quote, and the WIN-054 title is the strongest available demonstration that the dome occasionally uses anti-ΛCDM framing.

holes_found.description: Kernel-self-own redundancy persists on the WIN-048-is-labeled-X factual intro, even after EXP-428. Kernel h3 paragraph 1: "The WIN-048 prose for the CMB Axis of Evil is explicitly labeled 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' (data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence) — a sub-framing that admits the rhetorical structure is 'the alternative is in trouble,' not 'my model is right.'" Self-own h3 paragraph 2: "The WIN-048 prose for the CMB Axis of Evil is explicitly labeled by the dome as a 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction' — the dome's own framing acknowledges that the argument structure is not 'the dome predicts X' but 'ΛCDM cannot explain X' (data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence)." These are the same sentence with minor rephrasing — same subject, same predicate, same explanation of rhetorical structure, same citation. ISS-2135 (EXP-428) removed the category-error-narrative overlap ("the items are surfaced under the strongest-claim category...") but did NOT touch this label-introduction overlap. The decider may consider this resolved-enough-for-now if H1 forces a rewrite anyway (since the rewrite will likely consolidate the introduction), but if H1 is patched narrowly without restructuring, this redundancy will persist.
holes_found.recommendation: Resolved as a side-effect of H1 fix if Option A is taken — restructuring the kernel to use WIN-054's title naturally pushes the WIN-048-as-counter-prediction-style content into the self-own h3 (where it currently lives in paragraph 2) without kernel duplication. If H1 is deferred or fixed narrowly, the kernel could be restructured to cite the sub-label once and the self-own paragraph 2 could open with "The kernel noted that..." or simply drop the re-introduction.

holes_found.description: Kernel forward-pointer imprecision. The kernel says "The next two subsections trace why the closed-world commitments foreclose the dome side of any such argument; the fourth returns to why the placement-as-WIN of the rest is itself the self-own." But the immediately following h3 ("The list, and why list-match matters") is about borrowing-vs-independent-derivation, not closed-world commitments specifically. The closed-world dimensional argument lives in h3#3 only. The "next two" framing collapses two distinct argumentative moves (h3#2: import-not-derivation; h3#3: dimensional impossibility-of-derivation) under one umbrella label ("closed-world commitments foreclose"). Together they DO establish the no-dome-side conclusion, but only h3#3 invokes closed-world specifically.
holes_found.recommendation: If H1 fix triggers a kernel rewrite anyway, fold this in. Otherwise minimal edit: "The next subsection (h3#2) traces why the list-match alone is not corroboration without derivation; the one after that (h3#3) traces why the closed-world commitments foreclose the derivation route; the fourth returns to why the placement-as-WIN is the self-own." Slightly more cumbersome but accurate. Or terser: "The next two subsections trace why the import has no evidentiary weight without derivation (h3#2) and why derivation is foreclosed by closed-world commitments (h3#3); the fourth returns to why the placement-as-WIN of the rest is itself the self-own."

holes_found.description: c4 H2 dedup verbosity. The c4 H2 fix replaced the duplicate WIN-052 anchor with: "(see WIN-052 detail above and the no-derivation-chain argument earlier in this subsection)". This is accurate — the "no-derivation-chain" argument is in h3#2 paragraph 3 ("There is no derivation chain from the disc radius to RAR's a₀ threshold") — but it's heavy for a sentence-end parenthetical that follows a long em-dash clause. Curmudgeon c4 H2 had offered "just dropping the closing parenthetical" as Option B; the decider expanded the parenthetical textually instead. Both are defensible. Reading aloud, the closing reads as "...denied operates — and the dome has produced no derivation from firmament-projection mechanics that would substitute for it on any of these specific signatures (see WIN-052 detail above and the no-derivation-chain argument earlier in this subsection)." — three nested clauses by the end of a very long sentence. The closing parenthetical adds reading load without adding new information.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional minor edit: simplify the closing to "(see WIN-052 detail above)." The forward-reference to no-derivation-chain is helpful but readers who reached this point have already absorbed h3#2 paragraph 3's argument. Or drop the parenthetical entirely — the surrounding sentence already conveys the no-derivation-chain claim. Not blocking; the current version is correct, just verbose.

stronger_arguments.description: WIN-054's actual title "El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility" is a far stronger demonstration that the dome uses anti-ΛCDM framing in its own published page — the word "Impossibility" is in the dome's H3, the dome's prose explicitly says "El Gordo is the strongest single-object challenge to ΛCDM in existence," and the dome cites Asencio et al. for the framing. This is a verifiable dome quote, unlike the (false) "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-label. Rebuilding the kernel around WIN-054 turns the kernel-of-truth concession from "the dome had this category, used it once on WIN-048" (false) to "the dome uses anti-ΛCDM framing in its own WIN titles (WIN-054 explicitly), and that framing — which the dome's prose acknowledges throughout the MOND grouping — admits the rhetorical structure is 'the alternative is in trouble' as much as 'my model is right'" (verifiable).

The dome's audit.html publishes the canonical claim taxonomy as five classes. The kernel could note that none of those five classes is "my model + alternative-in-trouble" framed — the closest is "Retrospective structural" ("argument built after the fact from existing public data, but claimed to show a nontrivial geometric or field relation"). The dome's own audit doctrine says retrospective structural arguments should not be "counted as a pure forecast" — and the MOND-grouping items are all retrospective structural in nature. The dome's OWN taxonomy provides a sub-category (retrospective structural) that would be honest for these WINs, and the audit page explicitly says this class is not the same as prospective confirmation. That's the kernel-of-truth available without any false attribution: the dome's own audit doctrine provides the framework for distinguishing the MOND-grouping items from genuine confirmations, and the items are filed in the strongest category anyway.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author HAS made a public methodological move that acknowledges the difference between prospective claims and retrospective structural arguments. Audit.html publishes a five-class taxonomy and explicitly warns "do not treat every registry item as the same kind of evidence" (audit.html Current reading rule callout). The MOND-grouping items — which were added retrospectively from MOND-community published anomalies — fit the dome's own "Retrospective structural" class (audit.html Claim Classes table). The author had the conceptual machinery to file them differently, and the audit page documents that machinery in public view.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The author published the audit taxonomy on a separate page (audit.html) and continued to surface the MOND-grouping items as WIN entries on wins.html with badge CONSISTENT and badge CONFIRMED, contributing to the wins.html headline scorecard. The taxonomy exists; its application to the MOND-grouping items would have downgraded them; the application did not happen. The closed-world dimensional argument in h3#3 (data/sections.json p6-613-mond-grouping) still applies independently — even if the items were correctly filed as retrospective structural, the dome cosmology bounded at ~20,015 km disc radius cannot derive them from disc-cosmology principles.

text_fingerprint.verdict_summary_for_grouped_wins: WIN-047/048/052/054/055 → Self-Contradicted (sub-rationale: category-error import / MOND grouping)

deeper_analysis:
The EXP-428 mechanical tightening (kernel paragraph 185→135 words) is correctly executed and lands as advertised. The c4 H6 patch (69 Confirmed Predictions → confirmed-predictions) is clean. The c4 H2 patch (dedup WIN-052 anchor) is clean if verbose. These three patches close the curmudgeon c3 H1 / c4 H6 / c4 H2 holes mechanically.

The critical finding (H1 of this c5 review) is upstream of all three patches: the kernel's load-bearing "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-label is not a dome category. Verified by direct fetch of wins.html and audit.html (2026-05-19). The dome's WIN-048 is titled "CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction" with badge CONSISTENT; the dome's published claim taxonomy is five classes (Prospective confirmed, Retrospective structural, Supportive-nonunique, Pending-contested, Open refinement); the phrase "Counter-Prediction" appears nowhere in the dome's wins.html or audit.html.

The false attribution originated in data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence, which opens "The dome categorizes this as a 'ΛCDM Counter-Prediction'". That sentence is the upstream source. EXP-427 (which introduced the kernel-of-truth h3) and EXP-428 (which tightened it) both inherited the false attribution rather than auditing it. Multiple curmudgeon cycles (c1 through c4) did not catch it — including this curmudgeon's own c3 and c4 reviews, which audited the kernel's pacing and redundancy without auditing its factual premise. This is a textbook example of why citation-checking on each cycle matters: the redundancy work was technically correct but built on an unaudited foundation.

The section's overall verdict (Self-Contradicted for WIN-047/048/052/054/055) does not depend on the false sub-label. The closed-world dimensional argument in h3#3 stands on its own. The list-match argument in h3#2 stands on its own. The self-own argument in h3#4 can be reconstructed using verifiable dome quotes — WIN-054's actual title "El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility" is the strongest example of dome anti-ΛCDM framing in its own H3 text. The fix is scoped: rewrite the kernel h3 to use WIN-054 (or the audit-taxonomy framing) instead of the false WIN-048 sub-label, and correct data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence opening sentence.

Other-side counterargument I considered: maybe "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" was a dome sub-category at some earlier point (e.g., V51.0 in late March) and was reworded since. I checked raw-text/ (V50.6, pre-MOND grouping) and raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt (V51, includes WIN-064) — neither contains "Counter-Prediction". The phrase may have originated as an internal analyst paraphrase that hardened into a citable claim. Regardless of past dome state, the current published state is what the review claims about — and the current state does not support the attribution.

citations_verified: https://doi.org/10.3847/2041-8213/ad54b0 (Mistele 2024) — cited in h3#2 for RAR lensing extension. DOI format valid; not independently checked but corresponds to claimed paper.
https://doi.org/10.3847/1538-4357/ace62a (Asencio, Banik & Kroupa 2023) — cited in h3#2 for El Gordo 6.2σ framing. Dome itself cites Asencio et al. 2023 with the same framing on wins.html WIN-054.
https://doi.org/10.3390/sym14071331 (Banik & Zhao 2022 Symmetry) — cited in h3#2 for canonical MOND-supporting anomalies survey.
https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.117.201101 (McGaugh, Lelli & Schombert 2016) — cited in h3#3 for the original RAR paper.
https://john09289.github.io/predictions/coordinates.html — dome coordinates page, used in TLDR and h3#3 for disc-radius / firmament-height citations.
https://john09289.github.io/predictions/killshot.html — dome kill-shot page, used in h3#4 for the kill-shot taxonomy claim.
data/wins.json:WIN-052 / WIN-054 / WIN-055 — internal cross-references, exist in current wins.json.
citations_failed: data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence — cited as evidence that the dome labels WIN-048 "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction". The cite resolves (the data file does contain that text), but the upstream claim that the data file is making about the dome is FALSE per live fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html on 2026-05-19. This is the H1 critical finding. The data file needs correction, and the section's use of this citation needs reworking.
citations_unchecked: WIN-029 inconsistent firmament-height values cite (9,086 / 8,537 / 9,572 km) — h3#3 references WIN-029 internally; not re-audited this cycle.

summary_for_decider: c5 review: CRITICAL finding. EXP-428 kernel tightening mechanically correct (135 words, removes category-error preempt) and c4 H6 (69 → confirmed-predictions) + c4 H2 (WIN-052 anchor dedup) both landed cleanly. BUT — the kernel's load-bearing "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" sub-label citation is factually wrong. Direct fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html (2026-05-19) confirms: WIN-048 is titled "CMB Axis of Evil as ECM Structural Prediction" with badge CONSISTENT; the dome frames it as a positive ECM prediction (ΔT/T ∝ E_solar_circuit × cos(θ_ecliptic)); the phrase "Counter-Prediction" appears nowhere. Audit.html lists five canonical claim classes — no "Counter-Prediction" sub-category. The false attribution originates in data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence and was inherited by EXP-427 (kernel introduction) and EXP-428 (kernel tightening). Multiple cycles (c1-c4) missed it including this curmudgeon's own c3 and c4. Two-part fix: (1) correct data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence opening; (2) restructure the kernel h3 — recommend Option A, rebuild around WIN-054's actual title "El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility" which IS a verifiable dome anti-ΛCDM framing. The closed-world dimensional argument and self-own argument can be preserved with corrected attribution. Overall section verdict (Self-Contradicted for WIN-047/048/052/054/055) does NOT change — the structural argument is independent of the false sub-label. Recommended_action: major_rewrite (kernel h3 + 3 paragraphs of self-own h3 + wins.json:WIN-048 opening). Three other holes flagged: H2 moderate (kernel-self-own label-intro redundancy survives EXP-428; auto-resolved if H1 fix triggers restructuring), H3 minor (forward-pointer imprecision in kernel), H4 minor (c4 H2 patch verbosity).

advocate_mode.strongest_defender_response: A dome defender could point out: "Your review attributes a sub-label to my model that I do not use. The CMB Axis of Evil is filed on my wins.html as 'ECM Structural Prediction' with badge CONSISTENT — I claim ECM positively predicts the ecliptic alignment from solar circuit geometry. You're inventing a category ('ΛCDM Counter-Prediction') that I never published, then accusing me of inconsistent use of my own taxonomy. Either correct your review or withdraw the kernel." This is a real and specific objection that survives because the false attribution is factually checkable in seconds.
advocate_mode.our_strongest_counter: The closed-world dimensional argument (h3#3) and the list-match-without-derivation argument (h3#2) are unaffected by the kernel's sub-label error. Those two arguments are the load-bearing portions of the section. The kernel is rhetorical setup. The defender's "correct your review" critique applies to the kernel only — once the kernel is rewritten using a verifiable dome quote (WIN-054's "6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility" title, or the audit-taxonomy framing), the kernel function is restored and the substantive argument is intact. The defender does not get a free pass on the substance via the citation error.
advocate_mode.residual_risk: Until the kernel is rewritten, the section has an exploitable citation error. Severity depends on how publicly the section is read. Recommend immediate decider triage; this is the rare case where leaving the kernel in place for another review cycle is itself risky.

cross_win_consistency.notes: The MOND-grouping cluster (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055) all share the same Self-Contradicted verdict with sub-rationale "category-error import (MOND grouping)" per the section conclusion. Per-WIN reviews would benefit from a sweep: are any other WINs in the cluster citing "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" as a dome sub-label? Quick check of WIN-052 detail_evidence in data/wins.json would identify whether the false attribution propagated beyond WIN-048. Beyond this scope.
cross_win_consistency.follow_up_review_needed: Yes — analyst should audit WIN-047/052/054/055 detail_evidence opening sentences for similar false dome-sub-label attributions. Also: any other section that cites "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" as a dome sub-category needs the same fix. Quick grep across data/wins.json and data/sections.json should locate all instances.

quantitative_verification.delta_reconciliation: c4 H6 (-5 bytes) and c4 H2 (+45 bytes) net +40 bytes vs EXP-428-only, explaining the 43-byte discrepancy between predicted (17035) and actual (17078).
